title: quercus novomexicana var. typica
intro: Quercus novomexicana var. typica, also known as the New Mexican oak, is a plant species that belongs to the beech family and is native to the southwestern United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Quercus novomexicana var. typica include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function, among others. This plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although its uses and preparations should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified practitioner.
